message:
feat(e2e): implement timeout enforcement and enhance test harness

Adds proper timeout handling to the E2E test harness and extends the
common library with additional remote compilation utilities.

Timeout enforcement (harness.rs):
- exec_with_timeout() now actually enforces timeouts (previously TODO)
- Spawns process and polls try_wait() with 10ms intervals
- Kills process and collects output on timeout
- Appends timeout message to stderr for debugging
- Thread-based stdout/stderr collection to prevent blocking

Implementation details:
- Separate threads for stdout and stderr reading
- Non-blocking wait loop with early exit on completion
- Graceful kill() followed by wait() on timeout
- Exit code extraction with fallback for killed processes
- Duration tracking regardless of timeout

New helper methods:
- read_to_string(): Reads from pipe to String
- join_output(): Safely joins reader threads

Common library (lib.rs):
- Additional re-exports for test utilities

Remote compilation (remote_compilation.rs):
- Helper functions for remote compilation setup
- Worker connection utilities

This enables reliable E2E tests that don't hang indefinitely when
workers or the daemon become unresponsive, improving CI stability.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

     pub local_hash: BinaryHashResult,
     /// Hash result from remote build.

+    /// Time spent on local compilation in milliseconds.
+    pub local_build_ms: u64,
     /// Time spent on rsync upload in milliseconds.
     pub rsync_up_ms: u64,
     /// Time spent on remote compilation in milliseconds.

             success,
             local_hash,

             rsync_up_ms,
             compilation_ms,
             rsync_down_ms,
